name: Firstdraft
note: Australia's longest-running artist-led initiative, showing emerging and experimental art with regular public gallery hours in Woolloomooloo.

name: Airspace Projects
note: Artist-run gallery and studio complex in Marrickville presenting experimental and members' exhibitions.

name: Articulate Project Space
note: Artist-run project space in Leichhardt open to the public on weekends for experimental exhibitions.

name: 107 Projects
note: Redfern creative hub with artist studios, galleries and workshops open to the public.

name: Pari
note: Artist-run initiative in Parramatta showing contemporary work with public gallery hours.

name: The Cross Art Projects
note: Artist-run project space in Kings Cross with a regular public exhibition program.

name: Boomalli Aboriginal Artists Co-operative
note: Aboriginal artist-run co-operative in Leichhardt exhibiting and supporting First Nations artists, open to the public.

name: Parra Clay & Arts
note: Community ceramics and arts studio in the Parramatta area offering member access and workshops.

name: The Lock-Up
note: Contemporary art space in a former police station in Newcastle, open to the public.

name: WAYOUT Artspace
note: Artist-run contemporary art space in regional Kandos with public exhibitions.

name: Project Contemporary Artspace
note: Long-running artist-run gallery in Wollongong open to the public.

name: Red Point Artists Association
note: Artist collective with studios and gallery in Port Kembla, Wollongong.

name: Woodbine Cottage Pottery
note: Potter Helen Etough opens her Goulburn pottery for the Australian Ceramics Open Studios weekend.

name: Elph Ceramics
note: Sisters Sophie and Eloise Rankine hand-throw homewares at their Southern Highlands pottery, open by appointment Monday to Friday.

name: Flaming Gargoyle Pottery
note: Potter Alexander de Vos opens his Murrumbateman studio for the Australian Ceramics Open Studios.

name: Meg Patey Pottery
note: Long-established earthenware potter Meg Patey works from a large studio with electric and gas kilns in Colo Vale.

name: ANCA (Australian National Capital Artists)
note: Artist-run organisation in Dickson with member studios and a public gallery in Canberra.

name: Art Studios Cooperative
note: Cooperative providing working artist studios in Canberra.

name: Tributary Projects
note: Artist-run space in Fyshwick presenting contemporary and experimental exhibitions.

name: BUS Projects
note: Artist-led contemporary art organisation in Collingwood with a regular public exhibition program.

name: West Space
note: Long-running artist-run initiative in Melbourne's CBD showing experimental contemporary art.

name: Seventh Gallery
note: Artist-run gallery in Fitzroy/Collingwood supporting emerging artists, open to the public.

name: BLINDSIDE
note: Artist-run space in the Nicholas Building on Swanston Street with a public exhibition program.

name: KINGS ARI
note: Artist-run initiative in Melbourne's CBD showing experimental and emerging practice.

name: Trocadero Artspace
note: Artist-run gallery in Footscray open to the public.

name: Rubicon ARI
note: Artist-run initiative in North Melbourne with a public exhibition program.

name: Five Walls
note: Artist-run gallery and studios in Footscray open to the public.

name: Off the Kerb
note: Artist-run gallery in Collingwood presenting emerging artists.

name: fortyfivedownstairs
note: Not-for-profit gallery and performance space on Flinders Lane, open to the public.

name: Sticky Institute
note: Artist-run zine and self-publishing space in the Campbell Arcade, open to the public.

name: Blak Dot Gallery
note: Artist-run gallery in Brunswick focused on First Nations and culturally diverse artists.

name: Yarra Sculpture Gallery
note: Artist-run gallery in Abbotsford dedicated to sculpture, open to the public.

name: Arc Yinnar
note: Regional artist-run complex in Gippsland with studios, gallery and workshops.

name: Robyn Phelan Ceramics
note: Melbourne ceramic artist Robyn Phelan opens her North Melbourne studio during the Australian Ceramics Open Studios.

name: Minna Graham Ceramics
note: Ceramicist Minna Graham opens her Daylesford studio for the Australian Ceramics Open Studios.

name: Boxcopy
note: Artist-run contemporary art space in Brisbane dedicated to experimental practice.

name: Outer Space
note: Artist-run initiative in Fortitude Valley with studios and a public gallery program.

name: Kuiper Projects
note: Artist-run studios and gallery in Brisbane open to the public.

name: STABLE Artspace
note: Artist-run space in Brisbane presenting contemporary exhibitions.

name: Inkahoots
note: Long-running artist-run design and print studio in Brisbane.

name: The Walls Art Space
note: Artist-run contemporary art space on the Gold Coast, open to the public.

name: Umbrella Studio Contemporary Arts
note: Artist-run contemporary art organisation in Townsville with gallery and studio programs.

name: Reflex Projects
note: Artist-run project space in Toowoomba.

name: Darwin Visual Arts Association
note: Artist-run association in Darwin with studios and a public gallery.

name: Watch This Space
note: Artist-run initiative in Alice Springs presenting contemporary art in Central Australia.

name: FELTspace
note: Artist-run initiative in Adelaide's CBD showing emerging and experimental art.

name: Floating Goose Studios
note: Artist-run studios and gallery in Adelaide, open to the public.

name: The Mill
note: Multi-arts hub in Adelaide's CBD with artist studios, gallery and workshops.

name: Gray Street Workshop
note: Long-established contemporary jewellery and object studio workshop in Adelaide.

name: es ceramics
note: Working production pottery in Thebarton where the whole handmade process happens on site, open Thursday to Saturday with workshops.

name: PS Art Space
note: Artist-run gallery and studios in a Fremantle warehouse, open to the public.

name: Success
note: Artist-run contemporary art space in central Fremantle.

name: Cool Change Contemporary
note: Artist-run initiative in central Perth with studios and public exhibitions.

name: Leon Pericles Studio
note: Renowned printmaker Leon Pericles opens his Margaret River studio surrounded by a fantastical sculpture garden for the Region Open Studios.

name: Myles Happ Ceramics
note: Wheel-throwing potter Myles Happ shows stoneware at his Commonage Road studio during Margaret River Region Open Studios.

name: The Mermaid's Cave (Sasha Boundy)
note: Ceramicist Sasha Boundy makes whimsical pottery animals at her Busselton studio and cafe, open for the Region Open Studios.

name: Sawtooth ARI
note: Long-running artist-run initiative in Launceston with multiple gallery spaces open to the public.

name: Constance ARI
note: Artist-run initiative in Hobart presenting contemporary and experimental art.

name: Salamanca Arts Centre
note: Historic Salamanca Place warehouses house resident artist studios on the upper levels plus galleries, with regular open-studio events where visitors meet the makers.

name: Small Fires Studio
note: Ceramicists Isaac Patmore and Alby Holder open their Mornington studio for the Australian Ceramics Open Studios.
